title: DevKit sync --json output
description: Schema for the single JSON document emitted on stdout by `devkit sync --json` (and `devkit sync --dry-run --json`). See DevKit#11.

workspace_root.description: Absolute path to the workspace directory that `devkit sync` operated on.

overall_status.description: Aggregate status. `ok` iff every worktree outcome is clean. `partial` if any conflict/skip but no hard error. `error` if any fetch-failed/trunk-missing/rebase-error. `dry-run` for --dry-run invocations.

exit_code.description: Duplicate of the process exit code for caller convenience. 0 clean, 12 dep missing, 20 not in workspace, 21 sync partial.

repo.description: Short repo name (the worktree directory's name inside the workspace).

path.description: Absolute path to the worktree directory.

branch.description: Branch checked out in the worktree at the start of sync. Typically `issue-<repo>-<N>`.

trunk.description: Resolved trunk branch name (per `TRUNK.md` precedence; defaults to `main`).

outcome.description: Terminal classification for this worktree. `dry-run-plan` appears only when invoked with --dry-run.

commits_replayed.description: Commit count replayed onto trunk. Present only when outcome=rebased.

behind_count.description: Commits on origin/<trunk> that were not reachable from HEAD before sync. Equals 0 for up-to-date, and is coerced to 0 when fetch-failed (value unknown).

message.description: Human-readable detail or remediation hint. Required for non-clean outcomes; may be omitted for clean outcomes.

worktrees.message: rebase left worktree in rebase-in-progress state. Resolve conflicts and run `git rebase --continue`, or `git rebase --abort` to back out.
